sanskrit:
सिद्धिं प्राप्तो यथा ब्रह्म तथाप्नोति निबोध मे।
संसेनैव कौन्तेय निष्ठा ज्ञानस्य या परा॥
transliteration:
siddhiṃ prāpto yathā brahma tathāpnoti nibodha me |
saṃsenaiva kaunteya niṣṭhā jñānasya yā parā ||
literal_meaning: Perfection / having attained / as / Brahman / so / one attains / understand / from Me / in brief / indeed / O Kaunteya / the culmination / of knowledge / which / supreme
contextual_meaning: Krishna signals the end-game: the highest stability of mind is not an add-on but the natural outcome of doing one’s duty while internally relinquishing ownership of results. Psychologically, this is the shift from outcome-contingent self-worth to process-anchored self-consistency.
psychological_state: Preparation for closure; mind open to a concise protocol that promises ego-transcendence without abandoning worldly responsibility.
trigger_scenario: A high-performing professional on the verge of burnout who has chased targets for decades and now asks, 'Is there a way to keep working but feel internally free?'
anchor_text: Work wholeheartedly, let go of the outcome, and your mind rests in unshakable peace.
